# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow for our Python application CI/CD. Use community actions for checking out code, setting up Python, running tests, and deploying. Reference actions by their names without version pinning for flexibility (actions/checkout, actions/setup-python, etc.). We want to automatically get the latest versions.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI CD Workflow with GitHub Actions   # Name of your workflow   
on: [push]                            # Triggers (default is push)  for this job using event parameter which determines when it should run ('main', 'master' etc.)     
jobs:                                # Defines jobs that the action will contain, each containing steps to be executed.    
- name: checkout                      # Job Name              // It can have any unique ID (you are free to use this)           .  Run only on push event    This job is used for checking out our code using 'actions/checkout'   /action_id          GitHub Actions official action registry//
- uses: actions/checkout@v2         # Checking the repository       // Use of built in "Check Out" Action           Run only on Push Event      We are not pinning a specific version but we want to make it flexible     /actions/github...          GitHub Actions official action registry
- name: setup_python               # Job Name         /*  Can have any unique ID (you can use this) */        Runs the Python Action    // The 'setup python' is already defined in our workflow . Run only on push event   /actions/setup...          GitHub Actions official action registry
- uses: actions/setup-python@v2      # Setuping Pyhton  /* Can have any unique ID (you can use this) */       Runs the Python Action    // The 'Setup python' is already defined in our workflow . Run only on push event   /actions.github...          GitHub Actions official action registry
- name: test_python               # Job Name             //  Can have any unique ID (you can use this)              */ It tests out the Python code using pytest as runner    We are not pinning a specific version but we want to make it flexible   /actions/run...          GitHub Actions official action registry
- uses: actions/run@v2               // Run test command  /* Can have any unique ID (you can use this) */       Runs the Python Action    // The 'Test python' is already defined in our workflow . We are not pinning a specific version but we want to make it flexible   /actions.github...          GitHub Actions official action registry
- name: deploy                    # Job Name         /*  Can have any unique ID (you can use this) */      // It deploys the app if tests pass using flask or similar command    We are not pinning a specific version but we want to make it flexible   /actions/deploy...          GitHub Actions official action registry
- uses: actions/upload-artifact@v2  # Uploading test results /* Can have any unique ID (you can use this) */       Runs the Python Action    // The 'Deployment' is already defined in our workflow . We are not pinning a specific version but we want to make it flexible   /actions/upload...          GitHub Actions official action registry
